I actually enjoyed this as it reminds me why curiosity about the nature of things we know today is still important - [Curiosity Investigation - Forming A Concrete Reproducible Foundation]. Or

But like many scientific discoveries even though many have been fully investigated and validated over the centuries.

author: @RahereTheGenius
text:
It goes further back than the Leyden jars. Chest capacitors long predate that - in Spanish, Arcas. That gives a hint, what happens when you reduce the air insulation by lowering it to the ground? It arks out, goodbye Jericho.
Let's have a look at the science behind the retasking of the Escorial, in 1568, to be the alchemical Third Temple of Solomon. Professor René Taylor's Magia y Arquitectura tracks that back to Brussels, where Paul de Saint Hilaire tracks it in two directions, firstly to the alchemical refinancing of the Flemish cause by a certain Nicholas de Cerclaers, to the tune of 40 000 moutons d'or, which certainly existed, the receipt for the delivery of the funds is in the French Regional Archives in Lille. In the other direction, de Cerclaers escaped, but couldn't replicate the work away from Brussels. He eventually returns when things are safe, completely impoverished, and sets up shop next door to the Breughels, whose drawing is a warning. But within a year, he's bought his way into the Middle Class. And that's the year, 1618, when an unnamed alchemist destroys van Helmont's Paracelsian thinking by turning cold wet Mercury into hot dry Gold.
What you don't notice is that these are neighbours on the table of atomic weights. Annihilate a proton from Hg and you get Au. To get at the nucleus, strip the electron shells, which means plasma temperatures implicit in Dry Path process, if you apply conservation of energy to Wet Path process, wood-fired, taking a year. Dry Path took a month. So, 12 x 2-300C = 2400-3600C

author: @royksk
text: What a great opening. When I was on my honours, degree course for teaching design & technology (late starter age 47), part way through I learned that science had just stated that electricity flows the opposite way to what had been taught in all my years. That buggered my mind up; we still “designed” circuits the old way but they really worked in the opposite direction, WTF! We had a brilliant technician in the workshop who knew more than the lecturers and he told me that nearly all the circuits in text books were designed, not tested before publication and often didn’t work - how true.

author: @WhirledPublishing
text:
"Science" comes from the Latin meaning "knowledge" - "Science" refers to knowledge - if we ask 100 experts about electricity - or the history of electricity - we'll be told dozens of contradicting perspectives - if we ask 100 experts about the history of our Earth, we'll hear dozens of contradicting claims - in other words, "scientists" contradict one another.

If we want the history of Earth, we might ask a geologist or a volcanologist or a glaciologist or a paleontologist or evolutionary biologist ... they will give contradicting timelines for the events of Earth's past - if we ask Creationists, we'll hear more contradictions ... and yet, all of this is under the umbrella of "science" - many Creationists have PhD's - if you didn't know.

To discover the true timeline for the history of our Earth - which is not within the awareness of the public - are thousands of other sources which the public is programmed and indoctrinated and "educated" to disregard while these thousands of sources, documented in dozens of languages from all across our Earth, tell us the timeline for Earth's origins, Earth's expansion, Earth's broken and subducted tectonic plates, the timeline for Earth's continents, oceans, mountains, cataclysms, ice sheets, etc., as well as the timeline for the eruption of Yellowstone - and dozens of other supervolcanoes all in the same night as thousands of smaller volcanoes erupted across five continents - since the exact date of that horror is documented in different languages by people more than 16,000 km apart - and since that cataclysm is also corroborated by the Russians, the French, the Spanish, etc., that sailed upon the on-going horror and documented it, since our ancestors also tell us when and how the Yucatan Peninsula was formed, since they tell us when and why the giant "prehistoric" creatures suddenly appeared and rapidly disappeared from this Earth, since the skeletal remains of "prehistoric" creatures are found on or near the surface of our Earth while "ancient cities" are buried beneath several layers of debris, since our ancestors tell us when and how the massive water erosion occurred across North America and the British Isles - since they tell us the Ice Ages are fiction - since millions of seashells - and the skeletal remains of giant sea creatures - are found all across North America - and since glacial waters would have washed them away - those that enjoy guessing games from low IQ C-average lunatics that graduated from low level institutions with minimal entrance requirements are free to forget I mentioned the facts.

author: @davidblyth5495
text:
In some cases useful discoveries come from experimental results that were both undesired and unexpected.
A CEO of a dental implant company told us of how a particular metal came to be used.
For practice, the post-graduate medical students would implant metal screws into each other's femurs and later unscrew them.
In one case, they couldn't extract the implant and reported the problem to their professor.
Professor said that they didn't have problem but a solution - inadvertently they discovered an allow that would allow bonding with the bone.
I think it was with tungsten.

author: @Cryptic_Triptych
text: There is no such thing as "Free" energy. You can transform one form of electricity to another or generate energy to electricity, with losses. You can also induce or draw in energy to produce electricity. Electricity is a byproduct of Magnetism and Dielectricity.

replies.author: @Cryptic_Triptych
replies.text:
@poop.television PV (solar) for daylight hours, yes. If you have a few cloudy days, localized ground cover, dirt, snow, smoke, etc. you will not get enough kwhs to power your needs. Additionally, maintaining the system is always a concern. 

A much better system is atmospheric energy collection. 24/7/365, works better during inclement weather, solid state system, low cost set up and almost no maintenance.

replies.author: @Cryptic_Triptych
replies.text: @kevinleebailey  when helical chiral vorticies compress down to gamma against the Bloch wall, they phase shift into scalar hypertoroid and eventually collapse into a singularity. Phase shifting from plasma (the first phase state of matter) into dark matter/energy. This is what ~70% of the universe is made of. This is also known as The Aether, or as I have explained it, The Aetheric Fluid, because everything is Fluid Dynamics vibrating at a specific frequency. Therefore, my position is that we should be discussing Aetheric Fluid Dynamics and not merely plasma cosmology.

replies.text: @DerekRoss1958 so then one must provide a cost analysis of different ways to generate enough useful electricity (kwh) to operate the standard detached dwelling, roughly 10kw/day. Also to produce or store enough load to get through peak demand.

replies.author: @DerekRoss1958

author: @Cryptic_Triptych
text:
Repeatedly completing experiments and getting the same result. Within a reasonable amount of certainly. This applies to the physical realm - Natural Philosophy. 

However, science took a wrong turn. "The scientists of today think deeply instead of clearly. One must be sane to think clearly, but one can think deeply and be quite insane."
"Today's scientists have substituted mathematics for experiments, and they wander off through equation after equation, and eventually build a structure which has no relation to reality." - Nikola Tesla.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text: Great Quote!!  :0)X

replies.author: @amandapagett
replies.text: But anyone can do science. We do not have to be paid, we just need to write down observations systematically

replies.author: @Cryptic_Triptych
replies.text: @amandapagett indeed, the key word is systematically. One must have a consistent record of observation and reports. Additionally, one must have no previous biases, which is a difficult task to achieve. Thinking, expecting, wanting or being paid to produce a desired outcome is not scientific.
